title: Fixed-cell, microscopy-based evaluation of Ferritin accumulation in lysosomes
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5tnac","text":"Protocol for the fixed-cell, microscopy-based evaluation of Ferritin accumulation in lysosomes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Wash HeLa cells expressing doxycycline-inducible Parkin with 1x PBS
Add Trypsin to cells for 5 min and incubate at 37°C to dissociate cells from plastic well
Resuspend cells in 1 mL DMEM media
Count cells
Seed appropriate number of cells into 24-well glass bottom dish
Top up glass bottom dish with 1 mL DMEM and place cells back into incubator
Aspirate DMEM and fix cells in 1 ml pre-warmed 4% PFA for 30 min. PFA is toxic and discard in the appropriate waste streams.
Aspirate PFA solution and wash wells 3x with PBST (1x PBS, 0.02% Tween 20)
Permeabilize the cells by adding 0.2% Triton X-100 in PBS.
Remove the detergent solution by aspiration. Wash wells 3x with PBST (1x PBS, 0.02% Tween 20). Drain well.
Block cells for 10 min with 3% BSA – 1x PBS.
Remove BSA solution by aspiration. Wash wells 3x with PBST (1x PBS, 0.02% Tween 20). Drain well.
Incubate with primary antibodies in 3% BSA - 1x PBS for 3h at RT with gentle shaking. a. Anti-HA (rat) b. Anti-FTH1 (rabbit)
Wash wells 3x with PBST (1x PBS, 0.02% Tween 20). Drain well.
Incubate with secondary antibodies in 3% BSA - 1x PBS for 45 min – 1h. a. Goat anti-rabbit AlexaFluor 488 b. Goat anti-mouse AlexaFlour 647
Wash wells 3x with PBST (1x PBS, 0.02% Tween 20). Drain well.
Add SPY555-DNA for 1h in PBST with gentle shaking to stain nuclei
Wash wells 3x with PBST (1x PBS, 0.02% Tween 20). Drain well.
Exchange PBST with 1x PBS and keep cells at 4°C until imaging. Image within the next few days.

Perform image quantification was in your tool of choice. Here we will use ImageJ/FiJi and custom-written batch-macros and CellProfiler pipelines for quantitative evaluation. Use Fiji to create single-color gray-scale MIPs from each channel for CellProfiler. Import images into CellProfiler and assign them to cellular structures to be evaluated.
Segment nuclei, lysosomes, FTH1 and assign organelles back to nuclei in proximity, for per-cell evaluation of statistics.
Measure colocalization (Mander's Coefficient) between lysosomes and FTH1 for the images
Export measurements as .csv files.
Plot results in your tool of choice for graphing and statistical analysis.
Maintain cells until they reach appropriate confluency.
Stain cholesterol with Filipin (0.05mg/ml in PBS / 10% FBS) for 2h at RT. Wash cells after staining with PBST for 2 min.

Mount glass bottom plate on Yokogawa CSU-W1 spinning disk confocal on a Nikon Eclipse Ti2-E motorized microscope equipped with a Nikon Apochromat Plan Fluor 40x Ph2 DLL air objective. Image signals of 405/488/568/647 fluorophores in sequential manner with a Nikon LUN-F XL solid state laser combiner ([laser line – laser power]: 405 nm - 80 mW, 488 nm - 80 mW, 561nm - 65 mW, 640 nm - 60 mW]) using a Semrock Di01-T405/488/568/647 dichroic mirror. Collect fluorescence emissions with Chroma ET455/50m [405 nm], 488 Chroma ET525/50m [488 nm], 568 Chroma ET605/52m [561 nm], 633 Chroma ET705/72m [640 nm] filters, respectively (Chroma Technologies) using NIS-Elements image acquisition software. Consistent laser intensity and exposure times must be maintained for all samples. Acquire 8 µm z-stacks for each image.
12 randomly selected positions (8 µm z-stacks) were acquired were acquired using the HCA-module in NIS-Elements
creator: Felix Kraus
